headline: 'It is difficult to see what the French government could achieve in the coming year'
description: Constitutional law experts Olivier Beaud and Denis Baranger have studied the unraveling of France's executive branch, with a prime minister who is merely 'surviving' and a president isolated in denial over the end of his own time in office.

headline: Trump warns Iran blockade could last months, sending oil soaring
Jerome Powell's final run at the helm of the Fed
In Brussels, Hungary's Péter Magyar sets his sights on playing the European game
description: The incoming Hungarian prime minister met with Ursula von der Leyen on Wednesday, seeking to unlock European Union funds frozen over rule-of-law concerns under his predecessor, Viktor Orban. The total at stake is €17 billion, with an additional €16 billion earmarked to help the country rearm.

headline: Anger grows among London's Jewish community as antisemitic attacks surge
description: Two men, aged 76 and 34, were stabbed on Wednesday in Golders Green, a neighborhood home to part of London's Jewish community. Police described the assault, which followed a string of recent attacks on community institutions, as a 'terrorist incident.'

headline: Chanel's Matthieu Blazy brings a breath of fresh air to Biarritz
description: The artistic director unveiled his first cruise collection on Tuesday, April 28, in the Basque Country city where the house's signature style was born. The show was all elegance and lightness.

headline: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2': Product placement outshines plot
description: Released two decades after the original, the second installment stands out mainly for its parade of outfits, sure to delight fans.

headline: Gaza Freedom Flotilla says boats surrounded by 'Israeli military'

Trump blasts Powell staying on Fed board: 'Nobody wants him'

King Charles III urges US not to turn inward in speech to Congress
Marlène Laruelle: 'Orban's defeat is a particularly strategic loss for Russia'
The French love their public libraries. But lack of resources undermines the experience
description: France's unique network of 15,500 local cultural spaces, which are open to all, free of charge, has seen visitor numbers steadily increase, leading to certain issues.

headline: How Vietnam's garment factories benefited from Trump's trade policy
description: One year after the Trump administration introduced new tariffs, Vietnamese manufacturers of clothing and sports shoes are exporting more than ever to the US and Europe. But young people are turning away from these exhausting jobs.

headline: How Le Monde's London correspondent almost became a Reform UK candidate
description: After being approached multiple times, Le Monde's London correspondent recounts the questionable recruitment tactics of the far-right party. Her misadventure reflects Reform UK's efforts to try to take London from Labour on May 7.
